Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. South Polar Times 20 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Go on Embedded Systems and WebAssembly 136 upvotes, 18 comments
  3. Herbie: Automatically improve imprecise floating point formulas 60 upvotes, 9 comments
  4. Fake Fans 85 upvotes, 16 comments
  5. Build your own Dial-up ISP with a Raspberry Pi 124 upvotes, 29 comments
  6. iNaturalist 367 upvotes, 104 comments
  7. The FAA’s flight restriction for drones is an attempt to criminalize filming ICE 292 upvotes, 81 comments
  8. What changes when you turn a Linux box into a router 128 upvotes, 33 comments
  9. Artemis II crew take “spectacular” image of Earth 605 upvotes, 231 comments
  10. Run Linux containers on Android, no root required 57 upvotes, 17 comments
  11. We replaced RAG with a virtual filesystem for our AI documentation assistant 251 upvotes, 108 comments
  12. SSH certificates: the better SSH experience 229 upvotes, 101 comments
  13. Post Mortem: axios NPM supply chain compromise 212 upvotes, 94 comments
  14. Show HN: Travel Hacking Toolkit – Points search and trip planning with AI 39 upvotes, 13 comments
  15. The house is a work of art: Frank Lloyd Wright 61 upvotes, 24 comments
  16. Oracle Files H-1B Visa Petitions Amid Mass Layoffs 455 upvotes, 248 comments
  17. Delve removed from Y Combinator 132 upvotes, 63 comments
  18. Gold overtakes U.S. Treasuries as the largest foreign reserve asset 71 upvotes, 33 comments
  19. OpenClaw privilege escalation vulnerability 289 upvotes, 170 comments
  20. How to make a sliding, self-locking, and predator-proof chicken coop door (2020) 84 upvotes, 42 comments
  21. Tell HN: Anthropic no longer allowing Claude Code subscriptions to use OpenClaw 438 upvotes, 417 comments
  22. Bourbon waste could provide next-gen supercapacitor components 24 upvotes, 14 comments
  23. Age verification on Systemd and Flatpak 95 upvotes, 84 comments
  24. The Technocracy Movement of the 1930s 74 upvotes, 72 comments
  25. Big-Endian Testing with QEMU 82 upvotes, 82 comments
  26. An experimental guide to Answer Engine Optimization 5 upvotes, 2 comments
  27. Improving my focus by giving up my big monitor 50 upvotes, 66 comments
  28. Why are we still using Markdown? 79 upvotes, 145 comments
  29. F-15E jet shot down over Iran 424 upvotes, 969 comments
  30. Charge Robotics (YC S21) Is Hiring Software and Hardware Engineers 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 1:00pm, 4 April 2026. Source here.
